case_metadata.case_name: United Regional Health Care System AND Stuart Jackson Meyer, M.D. v. Timothy Hardy and Renae Hardy
case_metadata.court: Court of Appeals, Second District of Texas, Fort Worth

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: Hardy complained of extreme pain in the lower right quadrant of his abdomen, severe nausea, vomiting for two days, and fever.
evidence_index.summary: Patient presented with severe abdominal pain, nausea, vomiting, and fever.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: Meyer treated Hardy at Hospital. Meyer cancelled a urine culture that had been ordered for Hardy and ordered a plain (non-contrast) X-ray of Hardy’s abdomen.
evidence_index.summary: Dr. Meyer cancelled a urine culture and ordered a non-contrast X-ray.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: On December 30, Hardy, who was still in pain, went to the emergency room at a hospital in St. George, Utah, where he was diagnosed with a ruptured appendix.
evidence_index.summary: Patient diagnosed with ruptured appendix in Utah hospital.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: Hardy’s symptoms clearly required hospitalization and IV antibiotics. In addition, given Hardy’s symptoms, a contrast X-ray should have been ordered.
evidence_index.summary: Expert opined hospitalization and contrast X-ray were necessary.
evidence_index.speaker: Dr. William Frazier

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: We affirm the trial court’s order denying the motions to dismiss.
evidence_index.summary: Court affirms denial of motions to dismiss.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: We hold that this report represents a good-faith effort to state the causal relationship between the alleged breaches of the standard of care and Hardy’s injuries and is therefore sufficient as to causation.
evidence_index.summary: Court finds expert report sufficient on causation.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's analysis

verdict_detected.key_phrase: We affirm the trial court’s order denying the motions to dismiss.

initial_state.chief_complaint: Extreme pain in the lower right quadrant of abdomen, severe nausea, vomiting, and fever
initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Patient reports severe abdominal pain, nausea, vomiting for two days, and fever

reveal.result_summary: Imaging performed but findings not detailed in judgment

reveal.decision_impact: Should have been ordered to better assess the condition

decision_points.clinical_context: Patient in hospital with severe abdominal symptoms
decision_points.prompt: What imaging investigation should be ordered?

options.clinical_reasoning: Expert opinion indicated it was necessary given the symptoms
Less diagnostic value for suspected appendicitis

expected_action_court.description: Contrast X-ray should have been ordered
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Expert evidence established contrast X-ray was necessary

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Based on expert testimony that contrast X-ray was necessary
explanation.what_should_have_happened: Contrast X-ray should have been ordered to properly diagnose the condition

decision_points.clinical_context: Patient with severe symptoms requiring further management
decision_points.prompt: What should be the next step in management?

options.description: Admit to hospital and start IV antibiotics
options.clinical_reasoning: Expert opinion indicated hospitalization and IV antibiotics were necessary
Inadequate for the severity of symptoms

actual_action_defendant.description: Defendant did not hospitalize or start IV antibiotics

expected_action_court.description: Patient should have been hospitalized and started on IV antibiotics
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Expert evidence established this was necessary

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Based on expert testimony that hospitalization and IV antibiotics were necessary
explanation.what_should_have_happened: Patient should have been admitted and treated with IV antibiotics

timeline_phases.name: Initial Presentation
timeline_phases.description: Patient presents with severe abdominal symptoms

key_events.event: Patient reports severe abdominal pain, nausea, vomiting, and fever

timeline_phases.name: Diagnostic Workup
timeline_phases.description: Diagnostic tests and initial management decisions

patient_outcome.description: Patient diagnosed with ruptured appendix in Utah hospital

key_findings: Breach of duty in failing to order contrast X-ray
Causation established - earlier diagnosis would have prevented rupture

malpractice_determination.point_of_failure: Failure to order contrast X-ray and hospitalize
malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Contrast X-ray and hospitalization would have prevented rupture

factual_timeline.event: Patient presents with severe abdominal pain, nausea, vomiting, and fever
Patient diagnosed with ruptured appendix in Utah hospital

allegations.allegation: Failure to order contrast X-ray and hospitalize
